channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Orange Crush 2025: Georgia beach town in permit showdown over crime-fueled college fest
item.description: Tybee Island Mayor Brian West explains how the city plans to curb crime, traffic issues, litter and other problems during the Orange Crush spring break event in April.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Son of Chinese journalist jailed for espionage calls for his father's release
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Chinese journalist accused of espionage called for his father's release from a seven-year prison sentence in the high-profile case that signaled Beijing's tighten grip on journalism.
item.dc:creator: FU TING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope continues recovery, sleeps well as Vatican launches marathon prayers and allies cheer him on
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis continued his recovery from pneumonia Tuesday as the Vatican set in motion a nightly marathon of prayers from “his house” and allies cheered him on from afar in hopes that he might recover and get back to leading the Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Menendez brothers evidence not credible: LA County DA
item.description: "These new pieces of evidence add nothing to the self-defense argument," Hochman said Monday on "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about air traffic control towers after a midair collision in Arizona prompts questions
item.description: Tens of thousands of planes take off, land and perform touch-and-goes at the Marana Regional Airport in southern Arizona every year. Without an air traffic control tower, it's a calculated dance that requires communication by pilots.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 46.1 million people were counted in Iraq's first census in nearly 40 years
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — The final results of Iraq's first census in nearly 40 years released Monday show the population has reached 46.1 million. In 2009, an unofficial count estimated the population at 31.6 million.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Catholic community in Utah 'united in prayer' for the health of Pope Francis
item.description: The pope showed some slight improvements in recent tests, and as he continues to recover, members of the Catholic faith around the world — including in Utah — continue to pray for his health.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Merz prioritizes Ukraine and US, vows to tackle migration and economy after German election win
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's mainstream conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won the country's national election, ensuring that Ukraine has an even stronger supporter in the European Union's largest country and creating hopes for renewal in an economic powerhouse that has been badly battered in recent yea
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FCC Chairman climbs WKRG broadcast tower to highlight, promote industry
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, NewsNation

item.title: Major crafts retailer closing all stores
item.description: Less than two weeks after revealing it would close more than 500 stores, craft retailer JOANN says "going-out-of-business sales" will begin at all of its locations. JOANN filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in January, marking the second such filing by the company in less than a year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court declines to take up challenge to Tennessee law restricting drag shows
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to hear a challenge to a Tennessee law restricting some drag performances, allowing the first-in-the-nation law to remain largely intact.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: Princeton man accused of killing former star athlete brother, cat with golf club, knife near Ivy League school
item.description: New Jersey authorities have arrested Princeton man Matthew Hertgen, who is accused of killing his brother, Joseph Hertgen, and a cat, allegedly with a knife and golf club.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell suggests Trump has ‘gross misunderstanding’ of Ukraine talks
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) on Monday suggested that President Trump’s refusal to blame Russia as the sole aggressor who provoked the war in Ukraine “reflects a gross misunderstanding of the nature of negotiations and leverage.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Catholic bishops sue Trump over refugee resettlement suspension
item.description: “We are urging the government to uphold its legal and moral obligations to refugees,” said the executive director of public affairs of the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops.
item.dc:creator: Susie Pinto, NewsNation

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Police: Holland mom said she killed son to fulfill his death wish
item.description: A mom accused of stabbing her son to death told police she was helping him fulfill his wish: to die before his 18th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: Delta flight returns to Atlanta after possible smoke reported in flightdeck
item.description: A Delta flight returned to Atlanta on Monday after the crew observed a “haze” in the aircraft shortly after takeoff, the airline said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Judge rejects immediately restoring AP's access to White House but urges government to reconsider
item.description: A federal judge has refused to immediately order the White House to restore The Associated Press’ access to presidential events, saying the news organization had not shown it had suffered irreparable harm in the matter.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sedensky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Delta flight forced to return to Atlanta airport after 'haze' fills cabin
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight was forced to return to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport on Monday after a "haze" filled the cabin, the airline said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coast Guard detains 2 undocumented immigrants off Massachusetts coast
item.description: During a routine boat inspection on Thursday, the Coast Guard learned two of the six crew members were not U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 11 dead after supplemental shakes linked to listeria cases in 21 states
item.description: Frozen shakes sold to nursing homes and other institutions are being recalled after a listeria outbreak in 21 states that has caused 11 deaths, according to the Federal Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Johnston, Fox 5

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, KRON4

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Beware of text messages about unpaid tolls, FBI and state authorities warn
item.description: Drivers should be on the lookout for scam text messages warning of an unpaid road toll bill, according to recent warnings from authorities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, Fox 5

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro meets with family of fallen officer, victims of hospital shooting
item.description: "I am inspired by the amazing doctors, nurses, and staff at UPMC and WellSpan Health who did tremendous work to save lives in the face of tragedy," said Shapiro. "It was my honor to meet with them and to thank them for what they do in service to their neighbors.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, Fox 5

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
